# Opal

> Convert microblog posts from Twitter, Mastodon, Threads, and Nostr to Bluesky.

Opal is a free, open-source tool that converts a user's microblog post history from Twitter, Mastodon, Threads, and Nostr to Bluesky (app.bsky.feed.post records on ATProto). Original timestamps are preserved. Available as both a browser-based web app and a Node.js CLI tool.

## Supported platforms

- Twitter / X: Tweet archive (tweets.js). Supports tweets, retweets, and quote tweets with facets.
- Mastodon: ActivityPub outbox or CSV export. Supports content warnings, media attachments, and poll metadata.
- Threads: Meta data export. Supports text posts and media attachments.
- Nostr: Kind 1 text note events.

## What's converted

Posts with original timestamps, links/mentions/hashtags as facets, media attachments (images), reply threads (where resolvable), content warnings (Mastodon). Not converted: videos, polls, DMs, bookmarks, circle-only posts.

## Pages

- /: Landing page and feature overview
- /import: Import tool (OAuth sign-in and file upload)
- /about: Privacy details, supported platforms, OAuth scope, CLI usage, rate limit documentation, licence, and credits

## OAuth scope

`atproto transition:generic`

## Privacy

Runs entirely in the browser. Export files are processed locally and published directly to the user's PDS. No cookies, no local storage, no fingerprinting.

## Rate limit handling

Reads ratelimit-* headers, maintains a 15% headroom buffer, auto-adjusts batch size (up to 200 records), and respects abort signals immediately.

## CLI

Also available as a Node.js CLI. Clone https://github.com/ewanc26/pkgs, install dependencies in packages/opal, then run `pnpm start`. Key flags: -i (input), -h (handle), -p (app password), -m (mode: twitter/mastodon/threads/nostr/sync), -y, --dry-run.

## Licence

AGPL-3.0-only

## Author

Ewan Croft — https://ewancroft.uk
